Covers maintaining phone contacts for hearings and how appeal relationships are automatically created between appealed items and appeal cases, including multiple denied items. The main focus is the appeals events calendar: event entries are auto-created for appeal case and hearing lifecycle actions such as case creation, scheduling, adjournment, completion, approval/rejection, reopening, decisions, judicial review, appeal transfers, removals, and appealed item approval/rejection.

Overview of appeal/legal action maintenance: search cases by case reference and issues by case, issue reference, or client; manage items under appeal; maintain appellants with limits on removal; request hearing transcriptions only for multi-level hearing/hearing review/judicial review cases; and submit statements before the deadline. Also covers hearing phone contacts, automatic appeal relationships linking appealed items to appeal cases, and system-generated appeal events for case creation, scheduling, adjournment, completion, decisions, reopening, judicial review, transfers, removals, and approvals/rejections.

children.title: 1.9 Appeal and Legal Action Administration
Time Constraints
children.summary: This section explains appeal time constraints: configurable deadlines that control how many days are allowed for actions on appeal items, such as the maximum time to receive an appeal request. Administrators can update these values, and cases under appeal inherit the configured constraints. It also notes that time constraints can be maintained for each stage of a defined appeal process configuration.

children.title: Appeal Security
children.summary: Explains how appeal security controls access to appeal information and actions. It describes rights-based security for creating, reading, maintaining, and approving appeals, including approval of decisions. Access depends on case/product/participant security, location compliance, and sensitivity levels. Appeal security is configured via security hierarchy and appeal security administration using appeal SIDs (security identifiers) assigned to rights and user roles. A user can perform appeal operations only if their profile contains the relevant SID.

children.title: Appeal Process Administration
children.summary: Defines appeal process administration and security rights for appeals: create, approve, approve decision, maintain, and read. Explains that organizations can configure custom, multi-level appeal processes for applications, products, determinations, issue cases, and integrated cases, but only if the item is marked appealable. Each stage can restrict the appeal type (hearing case, hearing review, or judicial review) or allow any type. Describes active vs. canceled process status and future-effective processes that replace expired ones.

children.title: Clause Administration
children.summary: This section explains clause administration for decision documents and how standard clauses can be stored, searched, and inserted during document creation. It also covers administration of legal action types and categories, including defining legal action metadata, participant limits, home pages, case/investigation associations, and workflow events on create/complete. The legal action types are grouped into three main categories: Hearing, Petition, and Order, with sample hearing-related types listed.

children.title: Legal Action Types and Categories
children.summary: Explains how legal action types are configured in administration and inherited by case-specific legal actions. Covers defining a unique type name, allowing single or multiple participants, setting a home page, linking to integrated case/product case types or investigations, and associating workflow events on creation/completion. Also notes legal action types are grouped into three main categories: Hearing, Petition, and Order, with an example mapping hearing-related types such as Detention, Arraignment, and Sentencing.

children.title: Legal Statuses
children.summary: Explains configurable legal statuses within legal action administration. Legal statuses (e.g., temporary custody, parental rights terminated) are distinct from legal actions, can change over time, and their history is tracked for caseworkers. Administrators can associate statuses with case types, products, screenings, and investigations, and can remove those associations. The section also covers rules for whether one or multiple legal statuses may be active at once for a case type.

Overview of appeal and legal action administration. Covers appeal time constraints, rights-based appeal security (via SIDs and roles), configurable multi-level appeal processes with appealable/not appealable items, stage-specific appeal types, and active/canceled/future-effective process status. Also includes clause administration for reusable decision-document clauses. Legal action administration defines legal action types and categories, participant rules, home page settings, case/investigation associations, workflow events, and legal statuses with case/product/investigation associations and single/multiple active-status rules.

children.title: 1.10 Additional information
children.summary: This section is largely reference material. It lists related Merative/Cúram guides for further reading, then provides legal notices and licensing terms: permitted personal and enterprise use, restrictions on redistribution and derivative works, export compliance, warranty disclaimers, sample program copyright license, privacy policy link, and trademark notices. It is useful if you need supporting documentation or legal/usage terms rather than product functionality.

Overview of Cúram Appeals business concepts and related legal actions. Explains appeal purpose, roles (appellant, respondent, third parties), multiple-item appeals, and the three appeal types: hearing appeal, hearing review, and judicial review. Covers appeal levels, legal petitions/hearings/orders, and administrative setup such as appeal time constraints, security, clauses, legal action types/categories, and legal statuses. Also includes additional reference materials plus licensing, privacy, and trademark notices.
